raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Self-affirmation of values-based willingness; declarative commitment to prioritize evidence over fear-based avoidance",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Fear is not an infallible guide to action",
    "Evidence can reliably indicate whether an action is likely to be helpful",
    "Willingness is a choosable stance rather than a feeling",
    "The speaker experiences fear in relation to potentially beneficial actions"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent; distinguishes emotional response (fear) from evaluative criterion (evidence) without contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The individual can accurately evaluate evidence of likely benefit, the feared actions are in fact adaptive, and willingness functions as a workable alternative to avoidance",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Evidence assessment is biased or incomplete, the actions are not actually beneficial, or willingness is treated as a feeling rather than a behavioral choice"
}
